repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:

`%=` is documented as a separation point between alignment sections, but it was ignored inside fixed-width statusline item groups such as:

```vim
%20(foo%=bar%)
```

This also affected `rulerformat` when the ruler was rendered inside the statusline:

```vim
set ruler
set laststatus=2
set rulerformat=%20(%l,%c%V%=%P%m%)
```

In that case the left and right parts were rendered without the expected spacing.

Solution:

- Preserve the leading fixed-width group in `rulerformat` when it is rendered as part of the statusline.
- Handle `%=` locally inside fixed-width groups.
- Prevent separators inside groups from being processed again as top-level separators.
- Add a regression test for `%=` inside a fixed-width group.

Test:

```sh
VIMRUNTIME="$PWD/runtime" TEST_FILE=test/functional/ui/statusline_spec.lua make functionaltest
```

Fixes #39984

This PR is AI Assisted

comment_data.body: `spaces_to_add` is a character count , but you're using it directly as a byte offset in `STRMOVE`.
Good catch, thanks. I updated this to convert the character/cell count to a byte offset before using STRMOVE(), using schar_len(fillchar), and adjusted the item offsets accordingly.
only the first `%=` actually gets the spacing because of the break. So something like `%20(a%=b%=c%)` won't spread the space evenly across both separators the way a top level `%=` does
Ok! I updated the group-local %= handling to distribute the available space across all separators, using the same standard/final spacing approach as the top-level %= logic
logic is basically a copy of the top level separator handling around line 2038  Worth pulling it into a shared helper i guess.

Oki, make sense, I extracted the shared separator handling into a helper

Tested with:
`TEST_FILE=test/functional/ui/statusline_spec.lua make functionaltest`

comment_regular_data.body:
Sorry the only failing job is `ubuntu tsan clang functionaltest`, I think the failure is not related to this PR.

It failed in:
`test/functional/ex_cmds/swapfile_preserve_recover_spec.lua @ 140`

based on the quality of https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39964 , I'm not even going to bother reviewing this PR, since you didn't bother to read https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#ai-assisted-work
